Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education and professional knowledge, contributing indirectly to Targets 4.3 and 4.4.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Focuses on 'firm performance' and 'firm growth' (Target 8.2). 'CSR & Strategy' and 'Stakeholder Theory' support decent work and productive employment (Targets 8.5, 8.8).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Course covers 'Industry analysis' (Target 9.2). 'Dynamic Capabilities' and 'sustainability issues' relate to innovation and sustainable industrial processes (Targets 9.5, 9.4).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: 'Stakeholder Theory' and 'CSR' can be inferred to address inequalities by considering diverse stakeholder interests, but the link to specific inequality targets is weak.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Explicitly aims to 'understand... sustainability issues' and covers 'CSR & Strategy,' directly aligning with Target 12.6 on encouraging companies to adopt sustainable practices.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: 'Institutional theory' and 'Agency cost theory' relate to creating accountable institutions (Target 16.6). 'Ethical... issues' can be linked to reducing corruption (Target 16.5).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: 'Stakeholder Theory' and 'CSR & International Business' imply multi-stakeholder partnerships (Target 17.17), but the course focuses on firm theory, not partnership mechanisms.
